pattern: Once every character is mapped to a signed value, the best-cost substring is just a maximum-subarray scan: extend a running sum while it helps, reset when it doesn't, and never forget that the empty substring (cost 0) is always a valid fallback.
hints: First build a lookup of the value for every letter a-z: the override value if it appears in chars, otherwise its 1-indexed position in the alphabet.
Once every character has a numeric value, the problem is just 'find the maximum-sum contiguous run' over that array of values.
Keep a running sum that resets to a fresh start whenever continuing it would make things worse, track the best sum seen, and remember the empty substring (cost 0) always beats an all-negative run.

edge_inputs.description: one overridden letter drags cost down, best is the non-overridden run
every letter overridden to negative, empty substring wins
single character, no override, uses its default alphabet value
single character overridden to a negative value, empty beats it
entire string overridden to a negative value, must fall back to the empty substring instead of a negative total
a strongly negative character splits the string, so the best run is a partial suffix, not the whole string or a single character
no overrides at all, defaults to plain alphabet positions
positive islands separated by one deeply negative character, must pick the better island not the whole string

stress.description: long random string with a random subset of overridden letters and random signed values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    pool = list(letters)
    rng.shuffle(pool)
    k = rng.randint(1, 26)
    chars = ''.join(pool[:k])
    vals = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(k)]
    return [s, chars, vals]
